title: CHILD AND FAMILY SERVICES - 211info
text: NARA NW CHILD AND FAMILY SERVICES GRESHAM CHILD AND FAMILY SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides mental health assessment and evaluation, counseling (individual, couples, family, and group), case management, cultural groups and activities, Native parenting classes, foster parent groups, access to physical health services, Healthy Native Kids (OHP/CHIPRA) insurance assistance, and wraparound care for Native youth with multiple needs. Referrals to other resources provided. Also provides diapers. Staff receive training on cultural competence to provide cultural programming for clients. Staff includes Master's-level counselors and social workers, certified alcohol and drug counselors (CADC), psychologists and child psychiatrists, and nurse practitioners. Student interns studying to become CADCs or counselors may also work under supervision of licensed staff. The program is certified by the State of Oregon to provide mental health and addiction treatment services for all ages. 620 NE 2nd Street Gresham, OR 97030 Eligibility: Wraparound care: Youth who identify as Native American ages 9-21 All other services: Families with minor children who identify as Native American Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (503) 953-6598 Get Directions Visit Website Phone/FAX Numbers (971) 274-3757 Main phone (503) 953-6598 Intake Intake (503) 953-6598 Email Address: [email protected] INTAKE PROCEDURE: Call for information FEES: None DOCUMENTS: None required at intake AREA SERVED: Multnomah County Services: Adolescent/Youth Counseling * Native American Community Adoption and Foster/Kinship Care Support Groups * Native American Community Case/Care Management * Native American Community Cultural Heritage Groups * Native American Community Diapers Family Counseling * Native American Community Individual Counseling * Native American Community Mental Health Evaluation * Native American Community Navigator Programs * Native American Community Parent Counseling * Native American Community Parenting Materials * Native American Community Parenting Skills Classes * Native American Community Pediatric Developmental Screening * Native American Community Other Services Offered At This Location: SUBSTANCE USE DISORDER SERVICES - NARA NW YOUTH PROGRAM SUBSTANCE USE DISORDER SERVICES - YOUTH RESIDENTIAL TREATMENT PROGRAM If you represent this agency, update your listing here . * The community resource directory information is up to date to the best of our knowledge. However, you should always call the provider to confirm this information and make an appointment. Be sure to confirm payment information with the provider, if payment is required. 211Info does not rate, recommend or endorse any agency. We simply provide information as a public service.
